title: Regulatory & Framework Watch
description: Authoritative watchlist for the AI Model & System Assurance Control Matrix. Tracks: (1) frameworks already incorporated into the matrix with version verification metadata; (2) incoming regulations and frameworks under evaluation; (3) jurisdictions with active AI regulatory programs to monitor weekly. The regulatory-watch.mjs script reads this file and reports stale items and upcoming enforcement dates. The weekly-regulatory-watch GitHub Actions workflow runs the script and opens a GitHub issue with findings.

tracked_frameworks.name: EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689)
tracked_frameworks.authority: European Parliament and Council of the European Union
watch_for: Implementing acts under Art. 6(2) — additional high-risk AI use cases via Annex III expansion
Delegated acts — updates to Annexes I, II, VI, VII, IX, XI, XII, XIII
AI Office binding decisions and systemic risk designations (Art. 51 GPAI)
Codes of practice for GPAI providers (AI Office coordination)
European Standardisation Organisations (CEN/CENELEC) AI Act technical standards
Council formal adoption of Digital AI Omnibus amendments (pending as of 2026-06-26)
Member state implementation status and competent authority designations
AI Office guidance publications and Q&A documents

upcoming_milestones.event: Deadline for GPAI codes of practice initial finalization
upcoming_milestones.matrix_impact: gpai-provider and gpai-systemic-risk profile obligations; may clarify EV-03 evaluation protocol requirements
OA-09 (use-case classification) and LI-10 (applicability assessment) directly affected

upcoming_milestones.event: GPAI model obligations apply to models placed on market before 2025-08-02
upcoming_milestones.matrix_impact: gpai-provider required controls apply to legacy GPAI models not yet compliant

upcoming_milestones.event: Standalone Annex III high-risk AI system enforcement begins
upcoming_milestones.matrix_impact: eu-high-risk profile becomes enforceable; all required controls become pass/fail gates

upcoming_milestones.event: Product-embedded high-risk AI system enforcement begins
upcoming_milestones.matrix_impact: eu-high-risk profile for Annex I product-embedded systems

tracked_frameworks.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0
tracked_frameworks.authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
watch_for: NIST AI RMF 2.0 or update announcement
New AI RMF Playbook modules and sector profiles
NIST AI 600-1 GenAI Profile updates (separate document, planned as own catalog key)
NIST AI 100-4 (Synthetic Content) supplemental guidance
NIST AI safety institute publications and evaluations

tracked_frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 AI Management System
watch_for: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 amendments or corrigenda
ISO/IEC 42005:2025 AI system impact assessment (separate standard, complementary)
ISO/IEC 42006 (planned — AI auditing requirements)
SC 42 work items relevant to model assurance controls
ISO/IEC 12792:2025 AI transparency taxonomy (separate standard)

tracked_frameworks.name: Fed SR 26-2 — Supervisory Guidance on Model Risk Management
watch_for: Federal Reserve, OCC, FDIC, NCUA interagency guidance updates
OCC Bulletin 2026-13 supplements or FAQs
Fed supervisory letters on AI model risk (AI-specific supplements to SR 26-2)
CFPB AI guidance (adverse action explanations, fair lending AI)
CFTC, SEC, FINRA AI governance guidance for capital markets

tracked_frameworks.name: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025 v2.0
watch_for: OWASP LLM Top 10 2026 edition (annual cadence expected)
New vulnerability entries (LLM13+ agentic AI vulnerabilities)
Agentic AI supplement publication
Classification changes to existing LLM01-LLM10 entries

upcoming_milestones.event: Expected OWASP LLM Top 10 2026 publication (annual cadence — estimated)
upcoming_milestones.matrix_impact: LLM mapping requirement IDs in all 54 controls may need updating if classification changes

tracked_frameworks.name: OWASP AI Security Verification Standard v1.0
tracked_frameworks.authority: OWASP AI Security Verification Standard Project
watch_for: AISVS v1.1 or v2.0 announcements
AISVS requirement ID changes in a future release
License clarification (CC BY-SA 4.0 compatibility with matrix CC BY-NC 4.0)

completed_milestones.event: AISVS v1.0 formally released — commit f2bade20a5255a2f023e770784bd7b3cf1ebb599
completed_milestones.matrix_impact: aisvs source_status updated to current; version updated from 1.0-pre to 1.0; normative_force updated to voluntary-standard; AISVS_COMMIT_HASH GitHub secret set

tracked_frameworks.name: CSA AI Controls Matrix v1.1
watch_for: AICM v1.2 or v2.0 release
Validation of existing v1.0.3 control ID mappings against v1.1 (in-progress — 8 new domains need mapping)
CSA AICM EU AI Act mapping companion material
CSA AI-CAIQ (AI Consensus Assessments Initiative Questionnaire) updates
CSA STAR for AI program updates

upcoming_milestones.event: Target: complete validation of all control-level AICM ID mappings against v1.1
upcoming_milestones.matrix_impact: Stale v1.0.3 AICM IDs in control files must be updated or flagged as not-found in v1.1

tracked_frameworks.name: MITRE ATLAS v5.6.0
watch_for: MITRE ATLAS v5.7.0 or v2026.x release (maintains dual versioning post-May 2026)
New technique additions relevant to GPAI and agentic AI attack patterns
AML technique ID changes or deprecations affecting existing mappings
ATLAS agentic AI supplement or new tactic categories
MITRE ATT&CK AI-adjacent technique additions

watchlist.name: NIST AI 600-1: Generative AI Profile
watchlist.authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
watchlist.notes: Currently referenced via nist_rmf. Covers confabulation, IP, CBRN uplift, information integrity, compute — distinct coverage that does not map cleanly to base RMF subcategories. Planned as a separate catalog key primarily affecting EV and BH layer controls. Evaluate v1.0 content and determine which existing nist_rmf mappings should migrate to nist_ai_600_1.

watchlist.name: OWASP AI Testing Guide v1
watchlist.notes: Covers repeatable trustworthiness testing across EV/BH/CR layers. Status unknown — publication date unconfirmed. Primarily affects EV controls. Monitor for formal v1.0 release.

watchlist.name: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 AI Risk Management
watchlist.notes: Risk management framework companion to 42001. Aligns to CR and EV layers. Evaluate whether mappings would add material coverage beyond existing nist_rmf and iso_42001 mappings before incorporating as a 9th framework key.

watchlist.name: ISO/IEC 42005:2025 AI System Impact Assessment
watchlist.notes: Impact assessment lifecycle for AI systems, aligns to OA-layer controls (OA-02 human oversight, OA-08 contestability) and EV-09 risk classification. Evaluate whether high-impact-decision and eu-high-risk profiles should reference this explicitly.

watchlist.name: ISO/IEC 12792:2025 AI Transparency Taxonomy
watchlist.notes: Transparency taxonomy for AI systems — relevant to LI-04 (technical documentation) and OA-08 (notice and contestability). Low priority as standalone mapping framework; may be incorporated as a source citation within existing controls rather than a new catalog key.

watchlist.name: Anthropic Responsible Scaling Policy v3.3
watchlist.notes: Referenced in frontier-capability profile notes. Classified as matrix_thesis: true with thesis_type: frontier. Not a candidate for 9th framework key — changes too rapidly. Monitor for RSP v3.4+ that add new ASL tiers or evaluation protocols affecting EV-03 or EV-04 control content.

watchlist.name: Google DeepMind Frontier Safety Framework v3.1
watchlist.notes: Referenced in frontier-capability profile notes. v3.1 adds Tracked Capability Levels and Critical Capability Level for harmful manipulation; expands to cover model shutdown resistance. Monitor for v3.2+ updates. Not a framework catalog key — cited within specific frontier controls.

watchlist.name: UK DSIT AI Risk Framework
watchlist.authority: UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
watchlist.notes: UK pro-innovation AI regulation approach: existing sector regulators, not a new centralized AI Act. Key instruments: Bletchley Declaration, Seoul AI Summit commitments, DSIT AI Safety Institute (AISI) evaluations. Monitor for: binding DSIT sector guidance that creates enforceable obligations; AISI evaluation framework publications; AI Safety Summit follow-on commitments. Not currently incorporated — UK obligations differ materially from EU binding law model.

watchlist.name: Canada Bill C-27 / Artificial Intelligence and Data Act (AIDA)
watchlist.notes: AIDA is Part 3 of Bill C-27 (Digital Charter Implementation Act, 2022). Status: died on order paper when Parliament prorogued early 2025 — not enacted. Monitor for reintroduction in new Parliament session. Key obligations if enacted: high-impact AI system obligations (risk assessment, mitigation, monitoring, human oversight, incident reporting); general-purpose AI system transparency obligations. Relevant trigger controls if enacted.

watchlist.name: China Interim Measures for Generative AI Services
watchlist.authority: Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) et al.
watchlist.notes: Effective 2023-08-15. Applies to providers of generative AI services to the public within China. Obligations: security assessment before public release, algorithm filing, content labeling, training data sourcing legality, RLHF quality controls. Monitor for: CAC implementation guidance, new sector-specific AI regulations, updates to the AI governance framework. Relevant to gpai-provider and TG-03 (data rights) controls for organizations serving China.

watchlist.name: South Korea AI Basic Law
watchlist.authority: National Assembly of the Republic of Korea
watchlist.notes: Enacted 2024-12-27; expected to take effect mid-2026 after enforcement decree. High-impact AI systems: mandatory risk assessment, incident reporting, human oversight. General AI: transparency and labeling. Foundation model (AGI equivalent) provisions. Monitor enforcement decree for specific control obligations.

watchlist.name: Singapore IMDA AI Governance Framework / Model AI Governance Framework
watchlist.notes: Voluntary framework; agentic AI and generative AI governance guidelines in scope. Not yet binding. Monitor for IMDA enforcement legislative expansion or mandatory sector guidance in financial services / healthcare.

watchlist.name: Brazil Federal AI Law (Bill 2338/2023)
watchlist.notes: Approved by Senate committee 2024-12. Pending House vote as of 2026. Modeled partly on EU AI Act risk-based approach. Monitor for enactment and implementing regulations. High-impact AI systems would require risk assessment, human oversight, contestability — similar to eu-high-risk and high-impact-decision profile controls.

watchlist.name: ECB Guide to Internal Models v4.0
watchlist.notes: Supervisory guidance for capital models at ECB-supervised banks. Scope is capital models — not general enterprise AI. Informative sector reference for EV-08 (independent validation) in European banking context only. Not a candidate for framework catalog key. Reference within sr262 mapping rationale for EU banking context.

enforcement_calendar.description: Sorted view of all upcoming enforcement dates from tracked_frameworks and watchlist. Used by regulatory-watch.mjs to generate the upcoming events section of the weekly report.
enforcement_calendar.note: Dates here are maintained manually — they are not auto-derived from tracked_frameworks milestones. Keep in sync when tracked_frameworks.upcoming_milestones change.

events.event: Prohibited AI systems enforcement begins (Art. 5); GPAI code of practice deadline
Target date: complete v1.1 control ID mapping validation
Standalone Annex III high-risk system enforcement

github_checkable_sources.notes: Watch for formal v1.0 release tag — currently no release exists; content is in main branch only
Each ATLAS release updates technique IDs; compare latest release tag against current catalog version 5.6.0
Watch for 2026 edition; compare latest against current 2025 v2.0
